# SPDX-License-Identifier: Elastic-2.0
"""The 'gappy' suite: it only ever feeds a VALID password (and one too-short
one), so it never exercises the four character-class rules — a mutant that
drops, say, the digit requirement passes it undetected."""
import passwd


def test_valid_length():
    assert passwd.valid("Abcdefgh1!xy")


def test_too_short():
    assert not passwd.valid("Ab1!xyz")
detail.goal: valid must require length >= 12 AND an uppercase letter, a lowercase letter, a digit, and a symbol

detail.evidence: test_too_short asserts False for a password that is only 7 characters long. The code returns False immediately for any string with length < 12, making this test pass regardless of whether the character-class requirements (uppercase, lowercase, digit, symbol) are implemented. A mutant that removes any or all of the four character-class checks would still pass this test.

test_valid_length feeds a single password that happens to contain all four required character classes (uppercase, lowercase, digit, symbol). A mutant that drops any of the four character-class checks will still pass this test because the test string satisfies all requirements.

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Elastic-2.0
"""Tests for passwd.py valid() function."""

import passwd


def test_valid_positive_control():
    # Meets all requirements: length >= 12, upper, lower, digit, symbol.
    assert passwd.valid("Aa1!aaaaaaaa") is True


def test_length_below_12_with_all_classes_rejected():
    # 11 chars, has upper/lower/digit/symbol -> must be rejected (kills m1
    # which only enforces length >= 8).
    pw = "Aa1!bbbbbbb"
    assert len(pw) == 11
    assert passwd.valid(pw) is False


def test_missing_symbol_rejected():
    # length >= 12, has upper/lower/digit but no symbol -> must be rejected
    # (kills m2 which forgets to require a symbol).
    pw = "Aa1bbbbbbbbb"
    assert len(pw) == 12
    assert passwd.valid(pw) is False


def test_missing_lowercase_rejected():
    # length >= 12, has upper/digit/symbol but no real lowercase letter
    # -> must be rejected (kills m3 where uppercase incorrectly also sets
    # the lowercase flag).
    pw = "AAAA1111!!!!"
    assert len(pw) == 12
    assert passwd.valid(pw) is False


def test_digit_without_symbol_rejected():
    # length >= 12, has upper/lower/digit but no symbol -> must be rejected
    # (kills m4 where a digit incorrectly also sets the symbol flag).
    pw = "Aa1111111111"
    assert len(pw) == 12
    assert passwd.valid(pw) is False


def test_only_digit_present_rejected():
    # length >= 12, only digits present (no upper/lower/symbol) -> must be
    # rejected (kills m5 which uses OR instead of AND across categories).
    pw = "111111111111"
    assert len(pw) == 12
    assert passwd.valid(pw) is False
